title: Phishing
description: Hunt suspicious inbound email and downstream identity or alert activity that suggests phishing-driven access attempts.
hypothesis_templates: A phishing message initiated suspicious downstream identity or alert activity in the tenant.
Malicious email indicators correlate with unusual account access or user-reported compromise.

parameters.name: tenant
parameters.description: Tenant, business unit, or environment selector.

parameters.name: lookback_hours
parameters.description: Hours of email and downstream telemetry to review.

parameters.name: focus_sender
parameters.description: Optional sender or domain to prioritize.

telemetry_requirements.description: Email delivery, malicious message, or user-reported phish visibility.
Downstream alerts or events tied to users or infrastructure touched by a suspected phish.

blind_spots: Email content or trace fidelity may depend on tenant licensing and mailbox-level logging features.
User-reported phishing without preserved message context can limit message-to-session correlation.

execution_targets.name: M365 phishing alert and message review
execution_targets.description: Inspect email-related alerts and suspicious sender activity in Microsoft telemetry.

execution_targets.name: Sentinel downstream phishing correlation
execution_targets.description: Pivot from email-associated alerts into downstream user and host activity.
execution_targets.query_template: SecurityAlert | where TimeGenerated > ago({{lookback_hours}}h) | where AlertName contains "phish" or Entities has "{{focus_sender}}"

execution_targets.name: Splunk email-to-access pivot
execution_targets.description: Correlate suspicious email indicators with downstream access or alert activity in SIEM data.

notes: Use this pack when suspicious inbound email may have led to identity or endpoint follow-on activity.
